findings.evidence: `rg` shows this file has only `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_add_bounded_principal_self` at line 60; the parallel API has `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_add_principal_mass_self_on` at `TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/CoercivePerturbation.lean:122`.
findings.fix: Add `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_add_bounded_principal_self_on` with field hypotheses `hb`, `hc`, point `x ∈ Ω`, and conclusion for `energyIntegrand (a x + p x) (b x) (c x) U U`.
findings.issue: The explicit lower-bound lemma has only the pointwise `b₀ c₀` form, so coefficient-field consumers of the weak-form/Gårding estimate must manually rebuild the field specialization even though the neighboring perturbation API exposes it.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "grind_pattern" TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/BoundedPerturbation.lean` has no hits, while existing characteristic lemmas carry patterns, e.g. `UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:201` for `isCoercive_energyIntegrand`, `UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:224` for zero drift, and `CoercivePerturbation.lean:146` for perturbation coercivity.
findings.fix: Add `grind_pattern` declarations for `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_add_bounded_principal` and `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_add_bounded_principal_zero_drift` with patterns ending in the corresponding `IsCoercive (energyIntegrand ...)` conclusions.
findings.issue: The characteristic coercivity lemmas for bounded perturbations are not registered with `grind`, unlike the existing coercivity API, so users lose the expected automation path for the new public surface.

summary: The new perturbation surface is missing part of the characteristic API and omits the automation annotations used by the surrounding coercivity API.
